scenario: Nimbus Marketplace runs Camunda 8.8 SaaS for 24000 orders per day. After a deploy regression left 1500 order instances stuck on the same wrong service task, the operator needs to retry or cancel many of these process instances at once without scripting against the Zeebe API.
question: Which Operate capability lets the operator retry or cancel many process instances at once?

options.text: Selections in Web Modeler share a tagged set of orders with the operator, who then deploys a patched BPMN file to apply the retry or cancel action
Tasklist exposes a bulk select on user tasks that propagates the retry or cancel decision back to each parent process instance in the cluster
Selections in Operate let the operator pick a set of process instances and apply a retry or cancel operation to all of them at once on the cluster
Optimize emits a scheduled report that, when subscribed to, retries or cancels the matched process instances on the next cron tick from the cluster

a.text: Incorrect. Web Modeler authors BPMN files and does not provide selection-based runtime operations on process instances; retry and cancel at scale are surfaced by Operate selections.

b.text: Incorrect. Tasklist focuses on user task work and does not provide a bulk retry or cancel action on parent process instances; that capability lives in Operate.

c.text: Correct. The Operate introduction lists retrying or canceling many process instances at once via selections in Operate as one of the key operational features available to the operator.

d.text: Incorrect. Optimize is read-only analytics; it does not retry or cancel process instances. Bulk retry and cancel actions are performed in Operate via selections on the processes list.

explanation: The Operate introduction lists, as a key operation, the ability to retry or cancel many process instances at once via the selections feature on the processes list, alongside resolving incidents and modifying or batch-moving instances.
